inputs.userGoal: Deploy a real-time recommendation engine as an Azure ML managed online endpoint, scale for high request volumes, and store usage activity in Cosmos DB.

architecturePrinciples: Scalable — autoscaling managed online endpoint for high request volumes
Observable — full monitoring via Application Insights and Log Analytics
Secure — Key Vault for secrets management, RBAC authorization, soft-delete enabled
Cost-effective — pay-per-use Cosmos DB serverless not used to allow multi-region; Session consistency balances cost and correctness
Reliable — automatic failover on Cosmos DB, managed infrastructure for ML endpoint

reasoning.whyChosen: Workspace-based Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace. PerGB2018 is the recommended pay-as-you-go SKU for new deployments. Provides centralized log aggregation for all resources.
alternativesConsidered: Free tier — limited to 500 MB/day and 7-day retention, insufficient for production
reasoning.tradeoffs: PerGB2018 charges per GB ingested; cost scales with telemetry volume. 30-day default retention keeps costs low.

resources.name: appi-recommendation-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by the Azure ML Workspace for monitoring online endpoint latency, throughput, and errors. Workspace-based mode (backed by Log Analytics) is the recommended pattern; classic mode is deprecated.
alternativesConsidered: Classic (standalone) Application Insights — being phased out
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pricing is driven by the underlying Log Analytics workspace ingestion. No separate App Insights SKU.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace-based Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace as its backing store.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required dependency for the Azure ML Workspace — stores secrets, keys, and certificates. Soft-delete and purge protection are mandatory per ML Workspace pairing constraints. RBAC authorization is the modern approach over access policies.
alternativesConsidered: Premium SKU — adds HSM-backed keys, unnecessary for this workload
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier lacks HSM-backed keys. Sufficient for model secrets and connection strings.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required dependency for the Azure ML Workspace — stores model artifacts, experiment data, and logs. StorageV2 is mandated by ML Workspace pairing constraints. Standard_LRS provides cost-effective locally redundant storage.
alternativesConsidered: Standard_GRS — geo-redundant but more expensive, not needed for single-region deployment
Standard_ZRS — zone-redundant, good for HA but higher cost
reasoning.tradeoffs: LRS provides no geo or zone redundancy. Acceptable for model artifacts that can be re-uploaded.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for deploying custom model containers to managed online endpoints. Stores the Docker images used by the recommendation model inference server. Standard SKU provides 100 GiB storage — sufficient for model images.
alternativesConsidered: Basic SKU — 10 GiB storage may be tight for multiple model image versions
Premium SKU — geo-replication and private link not required for this deployment
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard lacks private link and geo-replication. Acceptable for a single-region deployment.

resources.name: mlw-recommendation-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Core resource hosting the managed online endpoint for real-time recommendation inference. Azure ML managed online endpoints provide built-in autoscaling, blue-green deployments, and monitoring — ideal for high request volume real-time serving. Basic SKU is sufficient for production ML workspaces.
alternativesConsidered: AKS-hosted inference endpoint — more operational overhead to manage clusters and scaling
Azure Container Apps — lacks native ML model management, experiment tracking, and MLOps integration
Azure AI Foundry Hub/Project — suited for multi-model AI orchestration, overkill for a single recommendation model
reasoning.tradeoffs: Managed online endpoints abstract away infrastructure but have per-instance-hour costs. Autoscaling mitigates over-provisioning.

resources.dependencyReasoning: ML Workspace requires Storage, Key Vault, and Application Insights at creation time (cannot be changed later). Container Registry is needed for custom model images used by the managed online endpoint.

reasoning.whyChosen: NoSQL API (GlobalDocumentDB) with Session consistency is ideal for storing high-throughput usage activity data from the recommendation engine. Cosmos DB provides single-digit-millisecond reads/writes, automatic indexing, and elastic throughput scaling. Session consistency ensures read-your-own-writes semantics at 1× RU cost.
alternativesConsidered: Azure SQL Database — relational model adds unnecessary schema rigidity for activity logs
Azure Table Storage — lacks global distribution, rich querying, and automatic indexing
Azure Cosmos DB Serverless — single-region only, no multi-region failover capability
reasoning.tradeoffs: Provisioned throughput requires RU/s capacity planning. Session consistency does not guarantee cross-session linearizability. Single-region deployment has no geo-redundancy — add secondary region later if needed.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture uses an Azure ML managed online endpoint for real-time recommendation serving with autoscaling, backed by a standard ML Workspace with all required dependencies (Storage, Key Vault, App Insights, Container Registry). Usage activity is persisted in Cosmos DB (NoSQL API) with Session consistency for low-latency writes. Log Analytics provides centralized observability across all resources. The design prioritizes operational simplicity — managed online endpoints handle scaling, load balancing, and blue-green deployments without requiring AKS cluster management.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Single-region (eastus) deployment — no geo-redundancy. Managed online endpoints cost more per compute-hour than self-managed AKS but eliminate infrastructure operational burden. Cosmos DB provisioned throughput requires upfront RU/s sizing, but autoscale throughput can be configured at the database/container level to handle traffic spikes.

plan.validation: All ML Workspace dependencies (Storage StorageV2, Key Vault with soft-delete, workspace-based App Insights, Container Registry) are correctly paired. Cosmos DB Session consistency is compatible with single-region provisioned throughput. No subnet or network conflicts — all resources use public network access in this default configuration. Container Registry Standard SKU supports ML Workspace integration. All resource names comply with CAF naming conventions and character constraints.
